div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Ranking mutually exclusive projects, OR
cash flows change sign more than once

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Communicating to an IRR-native /
non-finance audience (PE, ROI culture)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Liquidity / runway is the binding constraint
(startup, distressed, cash-rationed)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Projects of very different size,
capital rationed

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
LEAD WITH NPV.
IRR mis-ranks on scale/timing and can give
multiple/no IRR with sign-flips. NPV is additive

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
REPORT IRR alongside NPV, but let NPV
break ties. Sanity-check with MIRR when
reinvestment distortion matters

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
PAYBACK / discounted payback as a SCREEN only,
never the sole rule -- it ignores everything
after the cutoff. Gate, then decide on NPV

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
NPV for accept/reject; Profitability Index
(NPV / investment) to rank per dollar
